markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Record model provider/lane/local-cloud metadata for actions without leaking secrets or depending on provider memory. This MT belongs to ParallelSwarmStateRecovery. Make WP-009 safe for local/cloud swarm work, backend navigation, and repeated session recovery.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Local and cloud swarm lanes must be observable, attributable, bounded, recoverable, and safe for parallel agents.
Every MT must leave enough receipts, state markers, and next-step context for session-limit recovery by a no-context model.
scope.expected_behavior: LocalCloudAttribution leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: LocalCloudAttribution is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

commits.slice: Parallel swarm state recovery hardening for MT-209..216

evidence.summary: MT-209..216 implement lane/capability model, claim/lease receipts, mailbox/handoff/checkpoint/recovery atomicity, recovery/reclaim behavior, attribution scrub, and EventLedger-backed state recovery surfaces.

commands.result: PASS; 16/16 parallel swarm state recovery tests passed
PASS; test target registered without duplicate names

evidence.summary: Reviewer passed MT-209..216 after capability, lease, mailbox, receipt, handoff/checkpoint/recovery, and claim/release atomicity hardening.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-215 requires recording model provider/lane/local-cloud attribution metadata for swarm actions without leaking secrets and without depending on provider memory, with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code delivers this: a typed LocalCloudAttribution model, deterministic secret scrubbing applied on every persistence path, and JSONB attribution columns across five real migration tables, all proven against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL with round-trip secret-leak assertions.
findings: LocalCloudAttribution behavior is real: state_recovery.rs:133-177 defines mode (Local/Cloud/Operator/System), provider (OpenAi/Anthropic/LocalRuntime/OfficialCli/Other), runtime, model_label, credential_ref, provider_metadata. cloud() constructor scrubs metadata at construction (line 168) and scrubbed_for_persistence() (lines 172-176) re-scrubs before every DB write.

Secret scrubbing is deterministic and recursive: scrub_secret_metadata (state_recovery.rs:5235-5257) redacts keys containing secret/token/password/api_key or exactly 'key' to [REDACTED], recursing through objects and arrays. credential_ref stores only vault:// references, never raw secrets.

Every persistence path calls scrubbed_for_persistence() before serializing attribution to attribution_jsonb: claim (1364-1365), lease (2232-2233), handoff (2408/2569), checkpoint (2860/2886), recovery receipt (2956/2976), reclaim (3558). No raw-secret write path exists.

Real PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority: migration 0311_parallel_swarm_state_recovery.sql creates 5 tables each with attribution_jsonb JSONB NOT NULL and FK references to kernel_event_ledger(event_id); registers them in knowledge_schema_registry. This is Handshake-native PG, not SQLite (forbidden-tech grep on impl is clean).

Sub-rule 2 proof is real: tests use recovery_store() -> knowledge_pg() -> ManagedPostgres::ensure_running with isolated per-test schema, real migrations, pgcrypto (knowledge_pg_support.rs:108-128, base_database_url:39+). agent_lanes_and_work_claims_are_typed_attributable_and_exclusive (test:775) and mailbox_navigation_checkpoint_and_recovery_are_restartable_from_postgres (test:3815) round-trip through real PG and assert the serialized lane/recovery does NOT contain 'sk-test-secret' (lines 799-804, 3939-3944); receipt counts read back from DB (3946-3953).
Sub-rule 1 clean: no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/placeholder/mock/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-' hits in state_recovery.rs.
Sub-rule 3 clean: lifecycle.claimed_by=null, completed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260612, status READY_FOR_VALIDATION; not implementer self-cert, and proof is real independent of that.

Non-blocking paperwork asides only: implementation_commit e6aea0fc bundles MT-209..216 in one commit rather than an MT-215-isolated commit; packet cites v02.192 spec anchors vs current v02.194. Neither is a code/behavior defect.
evidence_checked: D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake Worktrees/wtc-kernel-009/src/backend/handshake_core/src/swarm_orchestration/state_recovery.rs (attribution model, scrub, persistence paths)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
